back: A small, fast memory located between the CPU and main memory that stores frequently accessed data and instructions to reduce memory access time.

front: Define cache hit and cache miss
back: Cache hit: when the requested data is found in cache. Cache miss: when data is not found in cache and must be fetched from main memory.

front: What are the three types of cache mapping techniques?
back: 1. Direct mapping: each block maps to one specific location. 2. Fully associative: any block can go anywhere. 3. Set-associative: blocks map to a set, within which they can be placed anywhere.

front: What is a Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
back: A hardware component that translates virtual addresses to physical addresses, manages memory protection, and implements virtual memory.
A memory management technique that uses disk space to extend apparent size of RAM, allowing programs larger than physical memory to execute.
A data structure maintained by the MMU that stores mappings between virtual page numbers and physical frame numbers.

front: What is a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
back: A special cache that stores recent virtual-to-physical address translations to speed up memory access.

front: Explain write-through and write-back cache policies
back: Write-through: writes go to cache and main memory simultaneously. Write-back: writes only to cache initially, main memory updated later when block is replaced.
The tendency of the processor to access memory locations that are close to recently accessed locations (e.g., sequential array access).
The tendency of the processor to access the same memory locations repeatedly within a short time period (e.g., loop variables).
The consistency of data stored in local caches of a shared resource; ensures multiple caches have the same view of memory.

front: What is the purpose of cache replacement policies?
back: To determine which cache block to evict when a new block needs to be loaded and the cache is full. Common policies: LRU, FIFO, LFU.

front: What is the difference between logical and physical addresses?
back: Logical (virtual) addresses are generated by CPU programs; physical addresses are actual locations in RAM. MMU translates logical to physical.

front: What is memory protection provided by MMU?
back: MMU can enforce access rights (read/write/execute) for each memory region and prevent unauthorized access to protected memory areas.
